current_state: Exact-row register exports and supplier/governance fields are public, but buyer-side procurement and page-specific public-assessment support remain unresolved on tested public routes.
threshold_event_that_counts: One public buyer-side tender, award, contract, or page-specific downloadable assessment companion that is explicitly tied to the same Amsterdam row becomes inspectable from an official/static public route.
why_this_event_changes_publication_state: This is the clearest single event that would close the main shard-006 blocker and let the package claim one exact-row municipal register case with both rich disclosure and buyer-side or assessment-side companion support.
what_does_not_count: Another mirror/materialization audit; another generic municipal search replay; richer register export/history alone; named-but-unattached assessment labels; cross-project Netherlands readiness notes without an Amsterdam row-linked public companion.
paper_thesis_use: Threshold contract for Result 6B / Table 1B / methods-results prose: defines the narrow event that would justify moving Amsterdam from register-rich caution row toward one exact bridged municipal exemplar.
visualization_dashboard_hook: Use as a route-priority table or blocker-status badge explaining why Amsterdam stays first in the shard-006 queue.
next_route_after_event_or_if_still_block: If the event lands, patch row-safe claim/caption/dimension/mechanism surfaces first; if not, keep Amsterdam first and avoid more packaging-only sync work.

current_state: Public same-buyer procurement continuity and IBM-consistent award metadata are visible, but the linkage remains family-level rather than exact page-specific proof for the parking-chatbot row.
threshold_event_that_counts: One page-specific buyer-side contract, tender-lot, award attachment, DPIA, or impact-assessment document explicitly tied to the parking-chatbot row becomes publicly inspectable.
why_this_event_changes_publication_state: This is the only event that legitimately unfreezes Helsinki from middle-state comparator status and converts the current family-level bridge into exact row-linked companion support.
what_does_not_count: More family-level procurement continuity; same-buyer or same-supplier consistency without row-specific tie; another wording-only defense memo; row-adjacent but not row-linked city documentation.
paper_thesis_use: Comparator-state contract for methods/results and thesis discussion of family-level versus exact-row linkage.
visualization_dashboard_hook: Use as a ladder-transition badge showing why Helsinki is stronger than register-only but still below exact row-linked closure.
next_route_after_event_or_if_still_block: If the event lands, Helsinki can become the first positive exact register-to-companion bridge; if not, keep Helsinki frozen and route effort to Amsterdam-first static public companions.

current_state: The UK rows already provide direct regulator-text governance depth, but they remain buyer-side thin and should not be reused as procurement or supplier proof.
threshold_event_that_counts: One official buyer-side procurement, supplier, downloadable DPIA, or public assessment companion appears for Serco or Chelmer and is explicitly tied to the same deployment context.
why_this_event_changes_publication_state: This would harden comparator diversity inside Table 1B, but it would not solve the main municipal exact-companion blocker by itself.
what_does_not_count: Reusing regulator text as if it were acquisition proof; generic school/workplace procurement context; another caption-only patch that does not change row evidence depth.
paper_thesis_use: Comparator hardening register for reviewer-response and mixed-surface methods framing.
visualization_dashboard_hook: Use as a comparator-upgrade state badge rather than as the main package blocker tracker.
next_route_after_event_or_if_still_block: If one event lands, patch comparator-side columns and rebuttals but keep Amsterdam/Helsinki route ordering explicit unless a municipal row also closes.

current_state: ORION currently functions as the official project-page low-trace floor and should not be reused as procurement-backed deployment or operational-effectiveness evidence.
threshold_event_that_counts: One official procurement, register, technical, or assessment companion appears that materially sharpens project-versus-deployment wording for ORION.
why_this_event_changes_publication_state: This would improve geography-side diversity and raise the floor above project-page-only visibility, but manuscript payoff remains lower than Amsterdam or Helsinki threshold closure.
what_does_not_count: Another route-order memo; generic border-governance context; translation-only polishing without a new official companion.
paper_thesis_use: Low-trace floor contract for appendix/methods and multilingual/public-sector bridge notes.
visualization_dashboard_hook: Use as a floor-state badge showing why ORION stays analytically useful but off hero/default views.
next_route_after_event_or_if_still_block: If the event lands, update floor/comparator routing; if not, keep ORION as the bounded low-trace control rather than a main route target.

current_state: The current package supports disclosure-surface heterogeneity and exactness-versus-traceability asymmetry, but not yet one fully bridged municipal register row with exact buyer-side or page-specific public-companion support.
threshold_event_that_counts: At least one already-seeded municipal register row crosses into exact row-linked buyer-side or public-assessment support while the existing caution layers are patched in sync.
why_this_event_changes_publication_state: This is the minimum package-level event that would justify moving shard-006 rhetoric from heterogeneity-only toward heterogeneity-plus-one-bridged-row, without overclaiming prevalence or transparency rankings.
what_does_not_count: A new sidecar alone; a route memo alone; a comparator-only upgrade; more local mirror-state cleanup; a new non-seeded row that lacks stronger support than the existing municipal core.
paper_thesis_use: Package gate for manuscript/thesis claim revision, reviewer memo updates, and dashboard legend tightening.
visualization_dashboard_hook: Use as a package-state legend row or manuscript blocker note explaining what exact event changes the claim ceiling.
next_route_after_event_or_if_still_block: Until this event lands, keep shard-006 work on bounded blocker tracking and do not widen procurement-transparency rhetoric.
